data.description: Addresses the colleague's surprise that rebar must be installed UNPACKED to disk. Rewrite the README 'Packaging note' (and docs/architecture.md) to explain WHY accurately: the engine is a bash dispatcher + helper .sh/.py files EXECUTED AS REAL FILES by an external bash interpreter (not a Python-import quirk), so standard wheel/pipx/brew/editable installs (all unpacked) work; only zipapp/zipimport/zip-safe bundling (Lambda zip, shiv/PEX) is unsupported, and the engine still requires bash on the host REGARDLESS of zip-safety. Reflect that library reads are increasingly in-process (native reads; Step-1 SSOT) but the write path + engine still shell out to bash. NOTE: prerequisite-list accuracy (flock optional/fallback, jq required, [mcp]/[dev] extras) already landed separately. AC: a reader hitting the unpacked-to-disk assertion finds a clear, accurate explanation; no overclaim of zip-safety.
data.title: #1 docs: rewrite packaging/portability note (resolve the 'must be unpacked to disk' confusion) + reflect native reads
